description:
Worktree branch names were inconsistent — agents produced agent-UUID, worktree-ID, bare ID, and ID/description variants. Tested adding the convention to prime.md workflow step 1.

## Result
Variant A (naming in workflow step): 2/2 pass. A single example in step 1 was sufficient. Agents produced `bw-6q6/route-changelog-through-md-pipeline` and `bw-4bv/filter-closed-blockers`. Variants B (start.md) and C (both) were not needed.

## Change landed
`1. **Worktree**: Create a worktree with branch `<id>/<short-description>` (e.g. `bw-a1b/fix-auth-bug`)`

Committed as 72fd826.

comments.text: Variant A (naming in prime.md workflow step 1) — 2/2 pass. Agent created: bw-6q6/route-changelog-through-md-pipeline, bw-4bv/filter-closed-blockers. Both match <id>/<short-description>. The one-line example in the workflow step was sufficient — no need for start.md or redundant placement.

title: Experiment: standardize worktree branch naming
